exemplar.title: How does wind speed change at different locations within a progressively narrowing street canyon?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does wind speed change at different locations within a progressively narrowing street canyon?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, accurate and effective communication of topic and purpose
Well-focused, concise research question tightly aligned with background and method
Detailed methodology including equipment, variables, procedure and safety
weaknesses: Methodology uses only one anemometer and lacks simultaneous measurements
Limited control of atmospheric variables (wind direction, turbulence)
Minor typographical issues in the derivation and notation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and research question) clearly meet the top band (5â6) descriptors, with effective focus and context. Methodology is solid but shows only partial evidence of informed design, placing it in the 3â4 band. Overall, a score of 5/6 reflects predominantly high-level performance with a single methodological limitation.

strengths: Accurate and consistent use of technical terminology (aspect ratio, viscosity, channelisation)
Appropriate application of core fluid-dynamics concepts (continuity, Venturi effect) in discussion and predictions
weaknesses: Occasional conceptual simplifications (assumption of laminar flow without Reynolds number justification)
Minor calculation slips (gust speed typo, viscosity discussion could be deepened)
Limited contextual discussion of Reynolds number and flow regimes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Terminology and concepts are mostly accurate (5â6 band), but subject knowledge application shows good rather than excellent depth and occasional oversimplifications (3â4 band). Averaging those performances yields a final 4/6 score.

strengths: Data are processed into means and uncertainties with clear graphs and error bars
Coherent argument sequence from background to conclusion
Evaluation acknowledges design flaws and proposes practical extensions
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely qualitative; lacks statistical hypothesis testing
Error-bar definitions and uncertainty propagation are not fully transparent
Discussion of day-to-day variability and gust quantification is missing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research analysis and argument development both fall into the âgoodâ 7â9 band, but critical evaluation sits in the 4â6 band. The lack of quantitative depth and inferential statistics limits the sophistication of the critical thinking. A holistic 4/12 reflects consistent but not fully developed critical analysis.

strengths: Clear and logical structure following IB conventions (title page, TOC, numbered sections, references)
Well-integrated tables, graphs and figures aiding comprehension
weaknesses: Inconsistent table layouts and some pixelated images
Minor notation artifacts and typos (Ï_eff, gust speed typo)
Colour choices in figures occasionally reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report adheres to IB presentation conventions with clear formatting and well-labelled visuals (band 3â4), though minor layout inconsistencies and image quality issues prevent a topâband (4) award. A score of 3/4 captures these minor but noticeable weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical termsâaspect ratio, mass-flow rate, viscosity, channelisationâare used **accurately and consistently** throughout narrative, tables and figures.  Equations are correctly formatted and referenced.  Minor typographical errors do not undermine conceptual accuracy, so performance aligns with the upper band (5-6).

The student builds a **coherent argument**: background â hypothesis â data trends â theoretical comparison â conclusion.  Logical sequencing and linkage to literature are evident; limitations are acknowledged, but some reasoning (e.g., gust interpretation) lacks quantitative support, preventing sophistication.  This aligns with the 7-9 band for discussion quality.

The methodology outlines **equipment, variables, procedure, safety and data-collection schedule** in enough detail for replication.  However, use of only one anemometer, absence of simultaneous measurements and limited control of atmospheric conditions show that design choices were *partially* informed; some controls (height, probe orientation) are justified, but others (wind direction, turbulence) remain weak.  The range of sources is adequate but not extensive.  These features fit the 3-4 band where methodology is âmostly complete with generally relevant methods and some informed selection.â

The student applies core fluid-dynamics concepts (continuity, Venturi, laminar vs turbulent, viscosity) appropriately to interpret field data and justify predictions.  Calculations (percentage error, velocity scaling) show **sound but not exceptional** grasp; occasional slips (e.g., â820 msâ»Â¹â gust typo) and simplifications (assuming laminar flow) limit depth.  Overall knowledge is good and mostly relevant, matching the middle band.

Section 3.4 provides a **balanced appraisal** of design flaws (single instrument, 2-D assumption) and suggests practical extensions (multiple anemometers, CFD).  Evaluation is thoughtful but episodicâsystematic critique of each variable and comprehensive error propagation are absentâplacing it solidly in the âgood evaluation with some critical analysisâ band (4-6).

The title, abstract-style introduction and section headings communicate the investigationâs purpose, context and scope with **consistent clarity and an appropriate scientific register**.  The student situates the study within urban wind-energy applications and explicitly links physical principles (continuity, Venturi, viscosity) to the street-canyon scenario, ensuring that the reader understands *why* and *what* is being investigated.  Minor typographical issues do not impede understanding; overall communication is accurate, purposeful and well-focused, meeting the descriptors of the highest band.

Tables, graphs and photographs are **well integrated**, labelled and referenced in text, aiding comprehension.  While a few images are slightly pixelated and some colour choices reduce clarity, visual elements generally support communication effectively, meeting the 3-4 band criteria.

A single, concise research questionââHow does wind speed change at different locations within a progressively narrowing street canyon?ââis **clearly stated and tightly focused** on a measurable dependent variable (wind speed) and explicit spatial conditions.  Subsequent background, hypothesis and method all align directly with this question.  While focused, the wording could specify the measurement height more explicitly; therefore the strand is placed at the bottom of the top band.

Data are processed into trial, daily and overall means with uncertainties; graphs and error bars support comparison, and percentage-error analysis links results to theory.  Discussion of wind-direction effects and vortex formation shows **clear relevance** to the RQ, though statistical depth is modest (no hypothesis testing).  This represents âgood research analysis clearly relevantâ, meriting the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The essay follows the IB EE structural conventions with title page, table of contents, numbered sections, references and appendices.  Headings are logical and pagination is present.  Minor layout inconsistencies in tables and some figure captions prevent full polish, but overall structure is clear and appropriate, justifying the higher presentation band.

annotations.comment: The viscosity section correctly describes wall adhesion but lacks discussion of the Reynolds number range in street canyons. Adding that context would deepen subject understanding.

The discussion of channelisation and the Venturi effect is clear but remains qualitative. Quantify the expected velocity increase by referencing the area ratio A1/A2 to strengthen the theoretical support.
The hypothesis combines both narrowing canyon and viscosity effects. Consider splitting into two clear, testable hypotheses to improve focus and methodological alignment.
The theoretical velocity is reported as 3.30 msâ»Â¹ but presented with unnecessary trailing zero. Use appropriate significant figures (e.g., 3.3 msâ»Â¹) to match data precision.
The percentage-error analysis is insightful and demonstrates clear linkage between experimental deviation and uncontrolled factors. This critical evaluation strengthens the discussion.
The evaluation acknowledges design weaknesses and proposes simultaneous anemometer use, reflecting thoughtful reflection on methodological validity.
Reflection on vertical wind profiles and turbine placement demonstrates high engagement and creative extension planning, linking physics to renewable-energy application.
The title is precise and communicates the investigationâs focus on variable-width street canyons clearly. This establishes a strong, well-defined starting point for the studyâs scope and purpose.

The research question is clear but omits a crucial detail: the measurement height of the anemometer. Specifying âat head height (â¼1.8 m)â in the question would strengthen its precision and alignment with the method.

Including the word count at the start meets the formatting requirement and shows adherence to IB conventions. Ensure this is paired with page numbering and title placement for a fully compliant front matter.
The introduction effectively situates the study within urban eolic contexts and outlines relevant physical phenomena, providing strong conceptual framework and motivation.

The introduction would benefit from explicitly referencing the research question to strengthen coherence between background and study aims. Link the stated phenomena directly to âHow does wind speed changeâ¦?â
Assuming laminar flow without discussing the Reynolds number threshold is oversimplified in an open street canyon. Include a calculation or citation to justify this assumption.
In the derivation, the symbol Q is introduced without definition. Clarify that Q represents volumetric flow rate or omit to use AÂ·v directly, to avoid notation ambiguity.
Reliance on a smartphone app introduces potential calibration and software errors. Calibrate against a known standard or include instrument accuracy specifications.
The variable description section contains an OCR artifact ("Ï_eff"). Correct notation and ensure all symbols are defined to avoid confusion.
The procedure is thorough but could be improved by using simultaneous measurements at all locations to eliminate time-lag and gust-induced biases.
Qualitative observations remain general (âfelt strongerâ). Incorporate specific gust counts or measured differences to anchor observations in data.
Uncertainties in Table 1 are calculated via range/2. A more robust statistical measure, such as standard deviation or standard error, would improve the assessment of data reliability.
Figure 7âs error bars lack definition of confidence levels or sources of uncertainty. Explicitly state how error bars were computed to enhance transparency.
The Day 3 gust value â820 msâ»Â¹â is clearly a typo or OCR error; verify the original source and correct to a realistic value (e.g., 8.20 msâ»Â¹).
Averaging daily trials into a single value (Table 3) obscures diurnal variability. Discuss how day-to-day fluctuations affect overall trends.
The discussion draws clear links between data and theory but omits statistical testing (e.g., t-test) to support significance of observed differences. Consider adding basic inferential analysis.
In the continuity calculation, explicitly note that height h cancels on both sides. This clarifies assumptions and aids reader comprehension.
References list shows varied source types but lacks consistent formatting (e.g., missing italics, retrieval dates). Apply a uniform citation style (APA or Harvard).
